name: Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. v. Attorney General of New Jersey
summary: The Third Circuit held that New Jersey AG administrative subpoenas are non-self-executing and that the AG must obtain a state court order before penalties can attach for non-compliance.
significance: The Third Circuit decision by Judge Hardiman that held New Jersey AG subpoenas are non-self-executing; the AG took the opposite position in that case but lost, and now relies on that holding.
